title: NOC 52110 – Film and Video Camera Operators | GTR Immigration
description: Film and Video Camera Operators under NOC 52110 are technical professionals responsible for capturing visual content for films, television broadcasts, news programs, live events, and digital productions across Canada. Classified under TEER 2, this occupation typically requires a college diploma or technical training along with hands-on experience. These professionals are commonly employed by:

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category5 – Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportTEER2 – Occupations usually require a college diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more yearsMajor Group52 – Technical occupations in art, culture and sportSub-Major Group521 – Technical occupations in art and cultureMinor Group5211 – Technical occupations in motion pictures, broadcasting and the performing artsNOC Code52110

duties: Film and Video Camera Operators perform some or all of the following duties:
Meet with directors and camera crew to determine filming sequences and composition
Select and set up camera equipment, lenses, and filters
Adjust focus, exposure, lighting, and camera settings
Operate film or video cameras to record events and productions
Operate drones or unmanned aerial vehicles for aerial filming (where applicable)
employment_requirements: Completion of a college or technical program in broadcasting, audio-visual technology, or a related field is usually required
Experience as an assistant camera operator is often required
A portfolio demonstrating creative and technical skills may be required
salary_prospects: Film and Video Camera Operators typically earn:
$37,800 – $77,000 per year,depending on experience, province, and production scale.
job_outlook: Growth in streaming services and online video platforms
Expansion of Canadian film and television production
Increased use of drone and digital cinematography
Corporate demand for professional video content
Strong opportunities exist in provinces such as Ontario, British Columbia, Alberta, and Quebec.
NOC 52110 is classified under TEER 2, which may qualify under:
Eligibility depends on work experience, language proficiency, and specific program criteria.

labour_market_demand: Growth in film and streaming productions
With more job openings than job seekers projected, employment prospects are considered strong.
Opportunities in film, television, and digital media
Career progression to director of photography or cinematography roles
Film and Video Camera Operators play a critical role in Canada’s growing entertainment and media industry.
